sgTip: After the Klikk fight, Tidus earns his first Sphere Level. Head straight for Cheer on his starting node — it stacks up to five times and reduces incoming physical damage for the rest of the early game.

subLocations.name: Submerged Ruins
subLocations.prose: Tidus surfaces alone in a flooded courtyard. Before triggering anything, sweep the area thoroughly. Head far west up the small stairs to find the Al Bhed Compilation Sphere (skip it on a first playthrough) and a chest with two Potions to the south. Cross to the far east platform for the partially hidden 200 Gil chest behind the Al Bhed sign. Follow the red arrow north to the bridge — grab the Hi-Potion by going west at the fork. After Geosgaeno forces you back, head through the southern doors and search the open drawer for the Flint. Then take the north doorway marked with a green square on the minimap, climb the stairs, and examine the dried flowers for the Withered Bouquet. Collect the Ether and second Hi-Potion from the upper passage on the way. The X-Potion chest is just right of the northwest locked door back in the central chamber. Save, then light the logs — Klikk ambushes shortly after. During the underwater salvage with Rikku, have her steal Grenades from every Piranha; these are your primary supply for both boss fights.

items.name: Flint
items.notes: Search the open drawer through the doors near the Save Sphere to the south (large campfire room).

items.name: Withered Bouquet
items.notes: Examine the dried flowers up the stairs through the north doorway (marked with a green square on the on-screen map).

items.name: Grenades (steal)
items.notes: Steal from Piranhas in the underwater ruins and directly from Klikk via Rikku's steal command. Stock up for the Klikk phase-change and Tros fights.

items.name: Grenades (steal — salvage dive Piranhas)
items.notes: During the salvage dive with Rikku, Piranhas attack in the second underwater chamber. Have Rikku steal as many Grenades as possible before Tidus kills them — these are your main supply for Tros.

items.name: Grenades (steal — Klikk)
items.notes: When Rikku exhausts her Grenade stock mid-fight, use Steal on Klikk directly to replenish. The guide specifically calls this out as a backup supply source.

subLocations.name: Salvage Ship
subLocations.prose: After the Klikk fight, Rikku's crew pulls you aboard. Talk to the soldier on the left for three Potions, then find Al Bhed Primer Vol. I in the northeast corner of the deck — easy to walk past. Chat with Rikku to unlock the Sphere Grid tutorial; open it now and spend Tidus's first Sphere Level on Cheer. When Rikku sends you on the underwater salvage, follow the chain down to the sunken ruins. Piranhas attack in the second chamber — have Rikku steal Grenades from each one before Tidus finishes them off, since these are your main ammo supply for the Tros fight that follows.

bosses.strategy: Scripted escape — this fight cannot be won or lost. Geosgaeno surfaces after consuming a Sahagin and immediately pursues Tidus underwater. Attack it three times; each of its counterattacks deals only fractional damage and cannot kill Tidus. After the third hit the sequence concludes automatically and Tidus escapes to dry land. No AP or loot is awarded. The HP figure in game data (32,767) belongs to the full rematch fought later from the Airship, where Geosgaeno is an optional boss with steals and drops — it does not apply here.

HP: 1,500. Tidus starts completely alone and cannot win solo — Klikk hits for roughly 180–200 damage per turn, which is lethal without help. Survive by guarding and using Potions until Rikku dives in mid-fight. Once she joins she uses Al Bhed Potions to heal the whole party and can use her Mix Overdrive if her gauge charged during the opening turns — let her act freely. After Rikku arrives it's a straightforward burn; just attack with Tidus while she keeps both characters alive.

Watch for Nautilus Charge: Tros retreats, then rushes — move to the Retreat position (corner prompt) to dodge it. Use Spiral Cut when available. Focus the body; arms regenerate HP if ignored but are a low priority. HP: 2,200.
